title: B.Com (Hons.) Financial Markets
content: Financial markets are vital to the smooth operation of economies. Financial markets refer broadly to any marketplace where the trading of securities occurs, including the stock market, bond market, forex market, and derivatives market, among others. This specialization is designed to understand the functioning of financial markets at an advanced level. It aims to equip the students with the knowledge and intellectual ability to examine and analyze the financial markets. The curriculum also includes certification courses from National Stock Exchange (NSE) Academy. This specialization is suitable for students aspiring careers as Finance Controller, Treasurer, Finance Manager, Credit and Cash Manager, Risk Manager, Insurance Manager, Financial Market Advisory, Money Markets Dealer, Financial Research Manager, Financial Planning Manager Trainee Associate, Financial Planning Consultant, etc. XII with minimum 50% marks in best four relevant subjects Mathematics/Commerce as a subject in XII level Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities:- 1) B.Com in Financial Markets Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level 01 Apply the knowledge of Accounting and Finance to solve real life business issues. 02 03 Analyze and interpret financial and non-financial information for Business Decision making 04 05 06 07 08 Imbibe professional ethics related to financial management practices. 09 Communicate effectively with business community and with society at large. 10 Explore business problems from an entrepreneurial perspective 01 Graduates will be able to demonstrate competency and leadership to become professional, employees and entrepreneurs leading to a successful career. 02 Graduates can pursue a relevant post-graduate study such as MBA, MBE, M.Com, MFC, CA, CS, ICWA and other professional courses. 03 Graduates will pursue lifelong learning in generating innovative solutions. 01 Make students ready for the self -employment and also for other career opportunities like Stock Broker, Investment Banker, Financial Advisor etc. 02 Develop and aware students to explore opportunities in the field of investment and share market. 03 Students can pursue higher education in the field of stock trading, finance & amp; risk management. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities:- 1) B.Com in Financial Markets Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) 50% or above in best four relevant subjects at XII level
